reviews.review: Playing as Grace is basically "Extreme Hide and Seek" where the seeker wants to eat your face. Then you swap to Leon, who is basically too tired to be scared anymore and just starts parrying everything with a hatchet. 10/10 Also can't wait to play tetris with my inventory.

Capcom... same old Capcom, always delivering. But this time, it’s different. They really nailed the vibes. The stress and that constant survival panic with Grace, then the pure adrenaline and "fury" with Leon... it’s a crazy mix. I knew it was going to be good, but honestly? Not this good. For the first time in years, a game actually blew me away. It’s not just about the graphics or the gameplay, it’s the whole feeling of it. Going back to Raccoon City and the R.P.D. station felt so special, especially for those of us who played the originals and the recent remakes. It’s that "good old days" feeling we’ve been missing for a long time. Couldn't have asked for more. Thanks Capcom, it’s been a hell of a ride.

As a fan of the series who has played every single game in the franchise I had high expectations from this game since it's like a closure to Umbrella saga. I can confidently say that the game surpassed all my expectations, there are things I love and some things I don't like about the game. I'll start with what I love. Also want to point out that there will be *spoilers* in this review so beware. First, as a more of a survival horror RE fan I loved Grace's gameplay. Rhodes Hill Chronic Care Center was very tense and so fun to explore. The zombies in this game keep some of their habits from their past lives and I love this idea and I also love how it was implemented in the game. There are zombies who hate noise who were patients, singer zombies, zombies that try and keep lights closed and maids who still try and clean the place while making a bloody mess lol. And they get angry when you kill a zombie near them thinking you made a mess that they have to clean. These kind of interactions with zombies are so funny and also unsettling. There are some bigger threats like the chef, chunk and the girl. And taking out these zombies don't mean they're gone. They can mutate into Blister Heads and trust me you don't want that. You can stop this mutation by using a Hemolytic Injector. This whole section was survival horror perfected for me. What I don't like about RHCCC is that I think the puzzles were way too linear. Everytime you find a key item you know where to use it and everytime you find something that needs some kind of passcode it won't take long to find it. I'm not saying that it should be so cryptic that you get stuck for hours but it could be more challenging than this. I also played on classic difficulty on my first run and I love the new mechanic where you have to craft ink ribbons with your resources, it adds a lot to the item management. Then there is Leon's sections and I also loved those. Before I start I want to point out that there are *spoilers* in this section of the review so be warned. The gameplay is basically RE4R but better. It's not so over the top like RE6. Exploring the ruined Raccoon City was a dream come true for me. I loved every second of it, finding easter eggs here and there was so fun and interesting. Right after entering Raccoon City we are welcomed by a giant spider that was missing from RE2R and from that moment I knew that so many more fan services were waiting for me in the city and I was right. From that point on the game literally throws so many fan service stuff in your face. Mr.X, HUNK, Plant 43, RPD, Kendo, Lickers, Nemesis, clone of Albert Wesker and as a fan of the series all of these made me excited like a little kid but the thing is these all happen too fast and feels rushed. Mr. X comes chases you for a few minutes then there is a boss fight and he's gone, Hunk comes to stop Leon has a fight with him and dies in a few minutes, you go through a cave and there is Plant 43 in there and dies a few minutes later. I wish these battles lasted longer and felt more fleshed out.I dont think Raccoon City's potential was wasted at all but it could have been better The Graphics are amazing and optimization is fantastic I love RE engine for that. In an era of gaming where every game has an issue with optimization this game feels like fresh air. Gameplay is so smooth and fun on both characters and animations are also really good. I've heard there are small bugs here and there but never happened to me so the game was very polished in my experience. I also loved the lore in the game. Learning the secret behind Raccoon City, what it had to do with Grace, what Elpis is and learning more about The Connections made me so excited. Some details in the story could have been executed better but still it's not so bad. The game is very replayable. With extra weapons, costumes, upgrades and challenges it gives you reasons to replay the game. Took me around 19 hours on my first playthrough but I took my time a lot. From what i've heard it's around 10-15 hours for most people which is enough with lots of replayability the game offers. The voice acting is also really good especially I loved Grace's voice acting. Overall, I loved every second I had playing this game and will be replaying it a lot over the coming days. If I have to rate the game I would give it a solid 96. It's one of the best if not the best Resident Evil game that came out since Code Veronica in my opinion.

This Dead Space remake isn't just a visual upgrade, it's a love letter to survival horror that proves some nightmares only get better with age. From the moment you step aboard the USG Ishimura, you're enveloped in an atmosphere so thick with dread that every corridor feels alive with malevolence. The Frostbite engine has transformed this derelict spacecraft into a breathing, groaning monument to cosmic horror. Every flicker of light, every distant scream, every shadow dancing across blood-stained walls conspires to keep your heart racing. The dismemberment system remains brutally satisfying, forcing strategic thinking with every shot. Resources are scarce enough to maintain tension but fair enough to reward exploration. Isaac now has a voice, and his vulnerability as an engineer thrust into hell makes the horror resonate deeper. The audio design is phenomenal: directional sound lets you hear necromorphs skittering through vents, creating genuine psychological dread as you frantically search for threats. What truly elevates this remake is its respect for player intelligence. No hand-holding, no immersion-breaking quest markers. You navigate using in-game tools, piece together the tragedy through logs, and survive through skill and nerve alone. The zero-gravity sequences are genuinely unnerving, and expanded side content adds tragic depth to the Ishimura's doomed crew. This is survival horror at its absolute finest: a haunting experience that understands fear isn't just about jump scares, but about sustained dread, isolation, and the primal terror of being hunted in the dark..

Dead Space is overall a very good sci-fi action horror game, that I enjoyed all the way through. The visuals are great, I love how the HUD is just Issacs suit, and the game-play and all the different weapons were fun. The dismemberment system is really detailed and gory. The story is interesting, But I do think it starts to drag near the end. I also noticed that the game does have micro stutters that happened the whole game but they never were that bad overall. Just a really memorable and awesome experience.
reviews.username: Single Mom at home with the kids

reviews.review: A couple complaints regarding performance and input (RTX 4070ti and Razer Naga Pro Wired): Light stuttering whenever a new room is being rendered and mouse input can slide like an ice skater when turning around. Besides those small issues, visuals are breathtaking, incredible story (especially the addition to Isaac Clarke's character depth compared to the original), great gun gameplay, awesome keyboard mashing moments, and badass boss fights in and out of the darkness of space. I do hope EA can gather their senses and fund the continuation of Dead Space's story with a Remake of Dead Space 2 and 3.
Friend roped me back into this game because of a joke...genuinely been having the time of my life. Fun as hell with friends who dont take it seriously.
its APEX legends, you've heard of it. Its good. takes some time to get good but once you do its so fun. Find a good team and get in.

I am 41 years old and probably one of the oldest players on Apex Legends servers. I have to say I enjoy this game a lot. I have been playing FPS games since the original Doom, and I played Unreal Tournament (UT) and Quake 3 a lot when they were launched on 1999. To me, UT is the greatest, and I played every iteration since then. However, lately I think Epic (UT developer) have gone for kid-friendly stuff with Fortnite to milk them and lost hardcore people like me in the process. In comes APEX LEGENDS. This is the sci-fi themed Battle Royale that I have been looking for. Excellent fight dynamics, very good character differentiation, good (but few) maps, great ping system and teamplay. I am married with children and still try to play a few games every night since I found this gem. And best part, it is FREE!! You should definitely give it a try.
10 bucks is an amazing price for the experience! Hopped in. Asked question. Got told to shut up. Guy who said shut up got shot and exploded. 10/10 Excellent game!!

Despite me having quite a few titles on my steam account where some of them have probably cost a fortune to develope - i must say that this game is among the most enjoyable games ive ever played with mates. Combining horror, teamwork and proximity chat is bound to be total success and it sure was with this cheap and awsome game. I highly recommend this one - just make sure you are a couple of mates playing and use the built in voicechat rather than external programs! (It's worth it!!)
A very nice game to play with friends or just queue up into a game with randoms. Very relaxing game and a great time every time I hop on

havent played this game in 2 years, decided to give it a try again, farmed the whole day, ignored family and friends for 12 hours.. just to end up getting 4 c4s thrown on my base and they were in my core within 2 mins of the raid.. getting called a bot on top of that... great game would do it all over

This game overall isn't so bad, it does though contain an issue. Every time I wake up naked on the beach, The urge to go to a forest in Japan with Logan Paul gets higher. In other words, this game sounds great to play and to have a great time with friends, but it's a simulation of endless torture where Satan himself whishes only agony upon your session. In conclusion, this game wants you to end up screaming in pain as if you were staring in a Saw movie. 10/10 - Would play again.
i started playing this game and i had a simple 2x2 and then a 8 man team came and blew me up and they were screaming racial slurs at me woul play again and recomend it to everyone
I come back to this game like going back to a toxic ex. Play a few games -> realise why I stopped playing -> take a break -> start missing the feeling -> repeat.

I have been playing this game on and off for over a decade now. I still remember playing the beta when they only had 1 killer and 4 survivors. My feelings towards this game always changes. There are times where I hate this game and times where I love this game. But the most important part is that I always end up coming back to play more. This game gets me addicted like crazy. No matter what anyone says about this game, The memories I have for this game will never go away. PS, They need to make a mutated spider/human hybrid killer.
This game is an abusive ex that I can't get rid of and anytime I do, I hopelessly come back for more. Traumatised. 10/10 game.

Diablo IV takes a lot of ♥♥♥♥. I guess I am basic. I like it. Do I know how to make a build? Nope. Do I spend endless hours searching for the best percentage on the perfect piece of gear? Nope. Couldn't care less. But I still manage to smash mobs and have fun. And the art and world are fantastic: gothic, dark, dreary, bloody, violent, miserable. I love it. Podcast/second-screen game to the max. Run around. Smash mobs. Have fun. Repeat.

I have never played any of the other games in the series so i cant say anything about how much better or worse Diablo 4 is. However i'm really enjoying this game, making a character is fun, loot and leveling is addicting and the combat is a huge spectacle 24/7. I know many players get very deep into making strong builds and min-maxing everything down to the smallest detail and there is certainly plenty of opportunity to do that in this game, however i am a much more casual player just exploring, going through the story and clearing dungeons with my friend and that has been VERY enjoyable so far. 8/10 Demon-babes 10/10 If you have friends
Ignore meta. Don't min/max. Save that for POE and Last Epoch. Just play Diablo 4. Take it easy. Let it be your casual, kick back and mindlessly relax game.
good combat loop and a new way to experience fromsoft mechanics. rough around the edges but really fun if you give it a shot

It is super fun until you defeat every boss and their everdark version. After that it's either self challenges like defeating bosses with certain nightfarers or at specific levels or it gets boring. I still highly recommend tho.

While this game does suffer from a lot of missed potential, ELDEN RING NIGHTREIGN and its DLC provide hundreds of hours of fun; it easily kept my friends and I hooked and grinding for months. The main idea of NIGHTREIGN is to condense the classic ELDEN RING experience down into a 45 minute run with randomized bosses, points of interest and weapons, and make it cooperatively playable up to 3 people. ELDEN RING NIGHTEIGN isn't exactly a roguelike, but it definitely plays like one. And a kind of weak one, at that. It's not a big deal at all when you first start playing, but becomes more apparent as you rack up hours. This game's biggest flaw is its lack of randomization in its experiences, leading to fatigue and runs ultimately not feeling significantly different from each other. This problem is helped by the addition of the DLC's content, but there are still many "What If?" moments. What if they could have added Shadow of the Erdtree weapons? What if there were twice as many legacy Dark Souls bosses? What if the POI's weren't so formulaic? If you are a massive roguelike fan, you may find other games in the genre to far surpass NIGHTREIGN's random elements. Basically everything else is so great though. The FROMSOFT combat and gameplay formula is more fun than ever, with new additions to the sandbox unique to NIGHTREIGN. The super sprint added brings the pace up to a blistering level and it's addicting to dart around the map. All playable characters are so much fun to play and master, with different costumes to acquire and relics to customize your loadout. All the bosses pose a feasible challenge and remain a blast to learn. NIGHTREIGN's best aspect are the ten final NIGHTBOSSES. They are completely original bosses with insane spectacle, challenge, and sense of reward for overcoming. Taking on each one and finally managing to conquer them alongside two friends still hasn't lost its luster. These are some of the greatest bosses FROMSOFT have ever created. Most of them have an alternate 'hard mode' version, with difficulty cranked to 11 and insane alternate movesets. Post launch, FROMSOFT added a "ranked mode" called Deep of Night, and it provides a massive challenge as well. This hard mode, alongside the added "hard mode" bosses offer tons of replayability. This game is an absolute blast, and will remain in rotation with my friends for a while. If you can't get enough FROMSOFT and you have a few friends willing to take the plunge, this is an easy recommend.
